prevalence.source: ISG (Indian Society of Gastroenterology) Consensus Guidelines
prevalence.trend: increasing due to urbanization and rising obesity

incidence.source: ICMR (Indian Council of Medical Research) Epidemiology Report

riskFactors: spicy foods prevalent in Indian diet
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV criteria adapted for Indian context: recurrent heartburn or regurgitation at least twice weekly, with consideration of dietary triggers like spicy foods

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis primary, endoscopy limited to alarm symptoms like weight loss or dysphagia
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: PPIs (pantoprazole, rabeprazole) widely available, lifestyle changes including reduced spicy food intake, Ayurvedic options (e.g., Sukumara ghrita for digestive balance)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Spicy diet culture exacerbating symptoms, limited endoscopy access in rural areas, urban-rural disparity in healthcare
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic PPIs, Ayurvedic integration for holistic management, ISG guidelines promoting evidence-based care
